title: Simple Majority vs Special Majority
rows: Definition
More than 50% of members present and voting in each House.
Majority of total membership (>50%) AND 2/3rds of members present and voting.
Article Scope
Technically outside Article 368 (e.g., formation of new states, quorum).
Strictly under Article 368 (e.g., amending Fundamental Rights, DPSP).
Approval Strength
Lower threshold, easier to secure in routine parliamentary business.
High threshold requiring intense cross-party consensus.
SSC Relevance
Often asked in context of legislative councils creation (Article 169).
Frequent numerical calculation questions in Tier-2 exams.

title: Special Majority vs State Ratification
rows: Requirement
Special majority in Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha separately.
Special majority in Parliament PLUS ratification by at least half of the state legislatures.
Subject Matter
Affects non-federal provisions like Fundamental Rights and DPSP.
Affects federal features (e.g., election of President, Supreme Court/High Court jurisdiction, GST Council).
State Role
No state legislature consent required.
Active consent from state assemblies (simple majority vote in each state).
SSC Relevance
Standard category for amending standard parts of the Constitution.
Favorite matching-type question testing federal provisions.

title: National Emergency vs President's Rule
rows: Trigger Grounds
War, external aggression, or armed rebellion.
Failure of constitutional machinery in a state (governor's report or otherwise).
Parliamentary Approval
Within 1 month by a Special Majority.
Within 2 months by a Simple Majority.
Maximum Duration
Indefinite (with parliamentary approval every 6 months).
Maximum 3 years (with parliamentary approval every 6 months).
SSC Relevance
Crucial distinction in approval timelines (1 month vs 2 months).

title: President's Rule vs Financial Emergency
rows: Trigger Grounds
Breakdown of constitutional machinery in an individual state.
Threat to financial stability or credit of India or any part of it.
Approval Timeline
Within 2 months by a Simple Majority.
Within 2 months by a Simple Majority.
Historical Invocation
Invoked numerous times across various states.
Never proclaimed in Indian history.
SSC Relevance
Frequent trivia question regarding Financial Emergency never being used.

title: Article 352 vs 356 vs 360
rows: Scope
Entire nation or specific parts.
A specific state government.
Entire nation or specific parts.
Majority Required
Special Majority.
Simple Majority.
Simple Majority.
Impact on Executive
Turns federal structure completely unitary.
State executive taken over by Governor / President.
Centre can direct reduction of salaries of public servants.
SSC Relevance
Core comparative mapping table for all three emergency types.

title: 42nd Amendment vs 44th Amendment
rows: Political Context
Passed during Internal Emergency by Indira Gandhi.
Passed by Morarji Desai's Janata government to reverse emergency excesses.
Nickname
Often called the 'Mini-Constitution'.
Known as the 'Restoration Act' / Emergency safeguards bill.
Key Additions/Changes
Added 'Socialist, Secular, Integrity' to Preamble; added Fundamental Duties.
Converted Right to Property to a legal right (Art 300A); added emergency safeguards.
SSC Relevance
Highest yield comparison in SSC CGL Polity sections.

title: Internal Disturbance vs Armed Rebellion
rows: Constitutional Status
Original term in Article 352 (misused in 1975).
Substituted term via 44th Amendment Act (1978).
Vagueness / Precision
Vague, broad, and open to subjective executive interpretation.
Precise, objective, and requires tangible proof of armed revolt.
Effect on Art 19
Could trigger suspension of Article 19 previously.
Armed rebellion alone does NOT trigger automatic suspension of Article 19.
SSC Relevance
Crucial conceptual shift tested under 44th Amendment modifications.

title: Before vs After 44th Amendment
rows: Emergency Advice
Prime Minister could advise President verbally or independently.
Written cabinet recommendation made mandatory.
Right to Property
Protected as a Fundamental Right under Article 31.
Shifted to a legal right under Article 300A.
Judicial Review
Emergency proclamation was immune to judicial review.
Made subject to judicial review against mala fide abuse.
SSC Relevance
Essential framework for checking democratic safeguards.

title: Fundamental Rights During Emergency
rows: Suspension Trigger
Automatically suspended during External Emergency (War / Aggression).
CAN NEVER BE SUSPENDED under any circumstance.
Presidential Order
Does not require separate order for Art 19 during external crises.
Protected permanently by 44th Amendment safeguards.
Enforcement in Court
Barred during the active window of emergency.
Enforceable in courts at all times.
SSC Relevance
Golden rule of SSC questions: Articles 20 and 21 are untouchable.

title: Basic Structure vs Constitutional Amendment
rows: Source
Explicitly defined text under Part XX (Article 368).
Judicial innovation established in Kesavananda Bharati case (1973).
Scope
Empowers Parliament to amend any part of the Constitution.
Places implied limitations preventing destruction of core features.
Inclusions
Fundamental Rights, DPSP, administrative frameworks.
Secularism, judicial review, federalism, free and fair elections.
SSC Relevance
Core conceptual balance between parliamentary supremacy and judicial review.
